name: Apple and Rum Custard Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak raisins in 1/4 cup rum for 30 minutes before using.
2. Mix flour, sugar, and lemon rind in a bowl.
3. Cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
4. Add egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk; mix gently to form a dough.
5. Press the dough into the bottom of a 10-inch Springform pan with only greased sides.
6. Mix bread crumbs and melted butter, spreading evenly over the crust.
7. Toss apple slices, lemon juice, and 1/4 cup sugar, spreading apples over the crumbs.
8. Drain raisins, reserving rum, then sprinkle over apples.
9. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
10. Bake for 15 minutes.
11. Beat eggs and sugar until thick and lemon-colored.
12. Stir in milk and reserved rum.
13. Pour custard over apples.
14. Bake for an additional 45 to 60 minutes at 350°F (175°C) until the custard is set.
15. Cool completely before serving. Do not remove springform pan until cool.
